title: Share of adults who are obese vs. daily protein supply
subtitle: Obesity is defined as having a body-mass index (BMI) equal to, or greater than, 30. BMI is a person's weight (in kilograms) divided by their height (in meters) squared.
caption:
Share of adults who are obese vs. daily protein supply

Obesity is defined as having a body-mass index (BMI) equal to, or greater than, 30. BMI is a person's weight (in kilograms) divided by their height (in meters) squared.

This scatter plot maps the relationship between adult obesity rates and daily protein supply for various countries in 2022. The vertical axis represents the share of adults who are obese, ranging from 0% to 70%, while the horizontal axis indicates daily protein supply in grams per day per capita, spanning from roughly 30 g to 150 g. Data points are color-coded by region, with North America in orange, South America in dark red, Africa in purple, Europe in blue, Asia in teal, and Oceania in light blue. Several Pacific Island nations, including Nauru, Tonga, and Samoa, occupy the upper section of the chart, showing obesity rates between 60% and 70% alongside protein supplies generally between 100 g and 120 g. The United States and Bahamas appear in the upper-right quadrant with obesity rates near 40% to 50% and protein supplies exceeding 120 g. In contrast, a cluster of African nations, such as Burundi, Rwanda, and Somalia, sits in the bottom-left area, characterized by obesity rates below 15% and protein supplies under 60 g. European countries like Iceland, Ireland, and Norway are positioned toward the right side with high protein supplies above 130 g but moderate obesity rates between 20% and 30%. Asian countries display a wide spread; China and Vietnam show lower obesity rates under 10% with varying protein levels, while countries like Kuwait and Qatar appear higher on the obesity scale. The "World" average point is located near the center, indicating an obesity rate just under 20% and a protein supply slightly above 100 g.
